_metadata.description: Anatomy → disease → protein mapping for the Human OS interface. Built from per-disease MCP queries (limit=500, min_score=0.0) against the cardiometabolic-research server, deduplicated by UniProt accession with each protein assigned to its highest-scoring disease primary organ. The top 47 hand-curated entries retain mechanism notes, hotspot residues, and variant counts; the rest are MCP-only.
caveats: Some larger diseases (CAD, dyslipidemia, hypertension, atherosclerosis, diabetes, obesity) hit the 500-record query cap; the very-lowest-scoring associations may be excluded.
Curated notes / variant counts / hotspots are present only for the original 47 top hits.
FH (Orphanet_406) returned empty; FH-canonical genes (PCSK9, LDLR, APOB) are surfaced under atherosclerosis or dyslipidemia.

proteins.name: Prothrombin
proteins.function: Thrombin, which cleaves bonds after Arg and Lys, converts fibrinogen to fibrin and activates factors V, VII, VIII, XIII, and, in complex with thrombomodulin, protein C. Functions in blood homeostasis, inflammation and wound healing. Activates coagulation factor XI (F11); activation is promoted by th
proteins.notes: Direct thrombin inhibitor target. Dabigatran mechanism.

proteins.name: Coagulation factor V
proteins.function: Central regulator of hemostasis. It serves as a critical cofactor for the prothrombinase activity of factor Xa that results in the activation of prothrombin to thrombin
proteins.notes: Factor V Leiden (R506Q) is the most common inherited thrombophilia.
